MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
Off Calendar Jan-04-2002 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: TRANSFERRED TO DEPT. 301 (170.6 CHALLENGE AS TO JUDGE ROBERTSON). (NR)

Bank Of America'S Motion To Strike The 3rd, 4th, And 6th Causes Of Action In The Second Amended Complaint
Off Calendar Jan-04-2002 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: APPEARANCES:
Andrew Livingston, Esq. and Heidi L. Swartz, Esq. of HELLER EHRMAN 772-6478 for Defendants and demurring/moving party
Charles A. Bonner, Esq. 331-3070 for Plaintiff and opposing party

The Court announced its tentative ruling as follows:
Cause of Action 1 -- granted -- no triable issue of fact re gender harassment.
Cause of Action 2 -- denied as to disability discrimination
Causes of ACtion 3, 4 and 5 -- sustained without leave to amend (barred by statute of limitations)
Causes of Action 6, 7 and 8 -- overruled.
Causes of Action 10 and 11 -- granted -- no triable issue of fact re at-will status.
Cause of Action 12 -- denied -- triable issue of fact re sexual orientation discrimination. Granted as to defendant MEYER.
Cause of Action 9 -- granted except as to the extent emotional distress damages are recoverable in conjunction with Causes of Action 2 and 12.

MATTERS WERE THEN ARGUED AND THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING WITH THE EXCEPTION THAT the motion re Causes of Action 2, 6, 7 and 12 was taken under submission.
(DAG/nr) Carol A. Karen, CSR 8199
